data.title: Is Korn Ferry (KFY) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Stagnation?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Korn Ferry's share price still offers value or if most of the opportunity has already been priced in, this article breaks down what the current market level might be telling you.
  • The stock recently closed at US$63.36, with returns of 1.4% over the last month, 1.4% over the last year, and 36.9% over three years, while year to date performance stands at a 4.6% decline.
  • Recent coverage around Korn Ferry has focused on its position in the Professional Services space and how investors interpret its long term prospects relative to peers. This backdrop helps explain why the share price has seen relatively modest moves over shorter periods, despite a stronger multi year return profile.
  • Korn Ferry currently holds a 5 out of 6 valuation score. The sections that follow will walk through the main valuation methods behind that figure, and will finish with a broader way to think about what value really means for this stock.

Korn Ferry delivered 1.4% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Professional Services industry.

Approach 1: Korn Ferry Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required rate of return. It is essentially asking what Korn Ferry's future cash generation is worth in today's dollars.

For Korn Ferry, the latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is reported at about $305.3 million. Based on analyst inputs for the near term and Simply Wall St extrapolations after that, projected Free Cash Flow for 2035 is $394.2 million, with intermediate yearly projections between 2026 and 2034 ranging from $308.0 million to $381.6 million. These projections are all in $ and are modeled using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach.

Rolling all of those cash flows into the DCF model results in an estimated intrinsic value of $133.17 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $63.36, the model implies the stock is about 52.4% undervalued on this cash flow view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Korn Ferry is undervalued by 52.4%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 62 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Korn Ferry.

Approach 2: Korn Ferry Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to connect what you pay per share with the earnings that support that price. It gives a quick sense of how many dollars investors are currently willing to pay for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk profile. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower multiple.

Korn Ferry currently trades on a P/E of 12.39x. This sits below both the Professional Services industry average P/E of 18.98x and a peer group average of 17.23x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Korn Ferry of 18.87x, which reflects factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margin, market cap and company specific risks.

The Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for these business characteristics rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. Comparing Korn Ferry’s current 12.39x P/E to the 18.87x Fair Ratio suggests the shares trade at a discount on this earnings based view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. On Simply Wall St you can use Narratives on the Community page to link your view of Korn Ferry’s story to a concrete forecast and fair value. You can then compare that fair value with the current share price to help inform your decision, and see it update automatically as new news or earnings arrive. data.title: Is Now The Time To Put KCTech (KRX:281820) On Your Watchlist?
data.description:
It's common for many investors, especially those who are inexperienced, to buy shares in companies with a good story even if these companies are loss-making. But the reality is that when a company loses money each year, for long enough, its investors will usually take their share of those losses. While a well funded company may sustain losses for years, it will need to generate a profit eventually, or else investors will move on and the company will wither away.

If this kind of company isn't your style, you like companies that generate revenue, and even earn profits, then you may well be interested in KCTech (KRX:281820). While this doesn't necessarily speak to whether it's undervalued, the profitability of the business is enough to warrant some appreciation - especially if its growing.

How Fast Is KCTech Growing Its Earnings Per Share?

Even modest earnings per share growth (EPS) can create meaningful value, when it is sustained reliably from year to year. So it's easy to see why many investors focus in on EPS growth. KCTech's EPS has risen over the last 12 months, growing from ₩1,987 to ₩2,358. This amounts to a 19% gain; a figure that shareholders will be pleased to see.

Careful consideration of revenue growth and earnings before interest and taxation (EBIT) margins can help inform a view on the sustainability of the recent profit growth. While we note KCTech achieved similar EBIT margins to last year, revenue grew by a solid 18% to ₩371b. That's progress.

Shareholders will be pleased by the fact that insiders own KCTech shares worth a considerable sum. We note that their impressive stake in the company is worth ₩175b. So there is opportunity here to invest in a company whose management have tangible incentives to deliver.

The combination definitely favoured by investors so consider keeping the company on a watchlist.
